pattern: Coins can be collected from up to two edges away without a dedicated visit, so peeling coin-less leaves and then peeling twice more shrinks the tree to exactly the part that must be walked, there and back.
hints: Branches that dead-end without ever reaching a coin never need to be walked at all -- repeatedly trim leaves that hold no coin until none are left.

Once every remaining leaf holds a coin, you still don't have to walk all the way out to it or back from it, because a coin within two edges can be collected in passing -- trim leaves two more rounds regardless of whether they hold a coin.
Whatever tree remains after that trimming must be walked completely, out and back, so the answer is exactly twice the number of edges left.

edge_inputs.description: a simple path with coins only at both ends
a branching tree with scattered coins
single node, no edges at all
no coins anywhere, nothing ever needs visiting
exactly one coin, so no travel is ever required
every node has a coin, the whole tree still collapses under the two extra trims
a star graph where every coin sits within collection range of the hub, so no travel is needed at all

stress.description: large random tree with random coin placement
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 30000))
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        parent = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        edges.append([parent, i])
    coins = [1 if rng.random() < 0.1 else 0 for _ in range(n)]
    return [coins, edges]
